  "text": "## LLVM IR Type System to Code Generation Chain\n\n### 1. Type Class Hierarchy\n\nThe base `Type` class is defined in `llvm/include/llvm/IR/Type.h`. It holds a `TypeID` enum (IntegerTyID, FunctionTyID, StructTyID, etc.) and a reference to `LLVMContext`. Derived types are declared in `llvm/include/llvm/IR/DerivedTypes.h`:\n\n- **`IntegerType`** (line 42): Extends `Type`, parameterized by bit width.\n- **`FunctionType`** (line 105): Extends `Type`, holds return type and parameter types.\n- **`StructType`** (line 218): Extends `Type`, holds element types (anonymous or named).\n- Also: `ArrayType`, `VectorType`, `PointerType`.\n\nImplementations of the factory methods (`IntegerType::get`, `FunctionType::get`, `StructType::get`) are in `llvm/lib/IR/Type.cpp`. These methods are uniqued: they look up or allocate from the `LLVMContext`'s internal storage.\n\n### 2. Type System Context Ownership\n\nThe `LLVMContext` class (`llvm/include/llvm/IR/LLVMContext.h`, implemented in `llvm/lib/IR/LLVMContext.cpp`) holds a pointer-to-impl `pImpl` of type `LLVMContextImpl*`.\n\n`LLVMContextImpl` is defined in `llvm/lib/IR/LLVMContextImpl.h`. It owns all type objects:\n- `DenseMap<unsigned, IntegerType *> IntegerTypes` — maps bit widths to uniqued integer types.\n- `FunctionTypeSet FunctionTypes` — uniqued function types.\n- `StructTypeSet AnonStructTypes` — uniqued anonymous struct types.\n- `StringMap<StructType *> NamedStructTypes` — named struct types keyed by name.\n- `BumpPtrAllocator Alloc` — arena allocator for all type objects.\n- Pre-allocated common types: `Int1Ty`, `Int8Ty`, `Int16Ty`, `Int32Ty`, `Int64Ty`, `Int128Ty`, `VoidTy`, `FloatTy`, `DoubleTy`, etc.\n\nWhen `IntegerType::get(C, N)` is called, it looks up `C.pImpl->IntegerTypes[N]` and if not found, allocates via `C.pImpl->Alloc`. Similarly for `FunctionType::get` and `StructType::get`. This ensures uniqueness within a context.\n\n### 3. IR Type to Machine Type Lowering in SelectionDAG\n\nThe lowering chain from LLVM IR types to SelectionDAG machine types:\n\n**Step 1: EVT/MVT — Extended/Machine Value Types**\n`llvm/include/llvm/CodeGenTypes/MachineValueType.h` defines `MVT` (Machine Value Type — simple types like `i32`, `f64`). `llvm/include/llvm/CodeGen/ValueTypes.h` defines `EVT` (Extended Value Type — wraps MVT and adds aggregate/LLVM type references).\n\n**Step 2: EVT::getEVT(Type*)** (`llvm/lib/CodeGen/ValueTypes.cpp`, line 301)\nConverts an LLVM IR `Type*` to an `EVT`. Handles integer types via `MVT::getIntegerVT`, floating-point types directly, and vector types recursively. This is the primary bridge from the IR type world to the machine type world.\n\n**Step 3: TargetLowering::getValueType(DataLayout, Type*)** (`llvm/include/llvm/CodeGen/TargetLowering.h`, line 1740)\nA wrapper around `EVT::getEVT` that additionally handles pointer types (lowering them to the native pointer size from the DataLayout) and vector-of-pointer types. This is the primary entry point for IR-type-to-EVT conversion in codegen.\n\n**Step 4: ComputeValueTypes / ComputeValueVTs** (`llvm/lib/CodeGen/Analysis.cpp`, lines 72 and 119)\n`ComputeValueTypes` recursively decomposes aggregate IR types (StructType, ArrayType) into their leaf scalar types, computing memory offsets. `ComputeValueVTs` calls `ComputeValueTypes` then maps each leaf `Type*` through `TLI.getValueType()` to produce a flat list of `EVT`s and optional memory `EVT`s. This is used throughout codegen (function lowering, call lowering, etc.) to flatten LLVM IR values for register allocation.\n\n**Step 5: SelectionDAGBuilder** (`llvm/lib/CodeGen/SelectionDAG/SelectionDAGBuilder.cpp`)\nThe main IR-to-DAG translation class. `SelectionDAGBuilder::getValue(const Value *V)` translates an LLVM IR value to `SDValue`(s). Inside, it calls `TLI.getValueType(DAG.getDataLayout(), V->getType())` to determine the machine type for each IR value, then creates SelectionDAG nodes accordingly."
}

## LLVM IR Type System to Code Generation Chain

### Type Class Hierarchy
- **`llvm/include/llvm/IR/Type.h`** — Base `Type` class with `TypeID` enum
- **`llvm/include/llvm/IR/DerivedTypes.h`** — `IntegerType`, `FunctionType`, `StructType`, `ArrayType`, `VectorType`, `PointerType`
- **`llvm/lib/IR/Type.cpp`** — Factory implementations: `IntegerType::get`, `FunctionType::get`, `StructType::get` (all uniqued via context lookup + bump allocator)

### Context Ownership
- **`llvm/include/llvm/IR/LLVMContext.h`** / **`llvm/lib/IR/LLVMContext.cpp`** — The `LLVMContext` class (pImpl idiom)
- **`llvm/lib/IR/LLVMContextImpl.h`** — `LLVMContextImpl` owns all types:
  - `DenseMap<unsigned, IntegerType*> IntegerTypes`
  - `FunctionTypeSet FunctionTypes`
  - `StructTypeSet AnonStructTypes` / `StringMap<StructType*> NamedStructTypes`
  - `BumpPtrAllocator Alloc` for all allocations

### IR Type → Machine Type Lowering Chain
1. **`llvm/include/llvm/CodeGenTypes/MachineValueType.h`** — `MVT` (simple machine types)
2. **`llvm/include/llvm/CodeGen/ValueTypes.h`** / **`llvm/lib/CodeGen/ValueTypes.cpp`** — `EVT` with `EVT::getEVT(Type*)` and `EVT::getTypeForEVT()`
3. **`llvm/include/llvm/CodeGen/TargetLowering.h`** — `TargetLowering::getValueType()` wraps `EVT::getEVT()` handling pointers
4. **`llvm/lib/CodeGen/Analysis.cpp`** — `ComputeValueTypes()` + `ComputeValueVTs()` flatten aggregate IR types
5. **`llvm/lib/CodeGen/SelectionDAG/SelectionDAGBuilder.cpp`** — `SelectionDAGBuilder` translates IR values to `SDValue`s using `TLI.getValueType()`

  "text": "## LLVM IR Type System to Code Generation Chain\n\n### 1. Type Class Hierarchy\n\nThe base `Type` class is defined in `llvm/include/llvm/IR/Type.h`. Type System Context Ownership\n\nThe `LLVMContext` class (`llvm/include/llvm/IR/LLVMContext.h`, implemented in `llvm/lib/IR/LLVMContext.cpp`) holds a pointer-to-impl `pImpl` of type `LLVMContextImpl*`.\n\n`LLVMContextImpl` is defined in `llvm/lib/IR/LLVMContextImpl.h`. It owns all type objects:\n- `DenseMap<unsigned, IntegerType *> IntegerTypes` — maps bit widths to uniqued integer types.\n- `FunctionTypeSet FunctionTypes` — uniqued function types.\n- `StructTypeSet AnonStructTypes` — uniqued anonymous struct types.\n- `StringMap<StructType *> NamedStructTypes` — named struct types keyed by name.\n- `BumpPtrAllocator Alloc` — arena allocator for all type objects.\n- Pre-allocated common types: `Int1Ty`, `Int8Ty`, `Int16Ty`, `Int32Ty`, `Int64Ty`, `Int128Ty`, `VoidTy`, `FloatTy`, `DoubleTy`, etc.\n\nWhen `IntegerType::get(C, N)` is called, it looks up `C.pImpl->IntegerTypes[N]` and if not found, allocates via `C.pImpl->Alloc`.
